title: Instruction Execution Steps
summary: Traces the complete 14-step sequence for executing a single instruction, from fetching the instruction address to writing the result.
content_points: Fetch: PC → MAR; issue memory read; instruction code → MDR → IR; update PC.
Operand fetch: load from memory (MAR/MDR) or select register.
Execute: load operands into ALU; ALU performs operation; result → ALU result register.
Branch: test condition; if true, load jump address into PC.
Write-back: result → memory (MDR/MAR/write command) or → destination register.
learning_objectives: Trace the instruction execution cycle step by step
Explain how each internal register is used during execution
